patch.value: Telemetry worker: no Postgres reconnect — ingestion stalls with events stuck in RabbitMQ after postgres restart

Incident 2026-06-09/10: pm-telemetry-postgres restarted; pm-telemetry-worker kept its dead connection and failed every insert with 'worker_insert_failed: the connection is closed' for ~24h. 15,778 events backed up in pm.telemetry.events (RabbitMQ retained all — no data loss); Postgres ingested only 11 events/24h. Manual 'docker restart pm-telemetry-worker' reconnected and drained the full backlog in ~2 min (24h count restored to 15,731). Fix needed in the worker (remote stack, scripts/prod/telemetry host): (1) reconnect/retry on closed Postgres connection instead of failing forever, (2) container healthcheck that fails on repeated insert errors so docker restarts it automatically, (3) optional: stack-health script should WARN when queue depth high while 24h ingest count is near zero.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Start telemetry worker reconnect and health hardening

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation start: duplicate-safe all-status search found pm-rj3w as the only active telemetry worker reconnect item; related historical telemetry and Windows teardown items are closed or verification-gated. Scope is production worker reconnect/health hardening plus operator validation, not a new tracker item.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Investigation note: the concrete worker reconnect/healthcheck fix surface is scripts/prod/telemetry, which is intentionally git-ignored as private production operations material. I validated the needed local shape (Postgres reconnect after insert connection errors plus worker health state and stall warning), but will not force-add private ops files to a public PR. Releasing this item for a private deployment-path change.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Pause public-repo work; private deployment path required
